questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The June 1774 meeting of Philadelphia freemen was called as a direct result of

choices.text: Great Britain's punishment of Boston for the destruction of British tea
the creation of an armed colonial militia in New England
pressure from loyalists to resolve tensions between Pennsylvania and Great Britain
establishing a committee to elect representatives to speak to the British king

questions.explanation: Correct answer: Great Britain's punishment of Boston for the destruction of British tea.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The Mayflower Compact (1620) and the Virginia House of Burgesses (1619) are early examples of

choices.text: resistance to British rule
economic systems in the colonies
steps toward representative government
restrictions on religious freedom

questions.explanation: Correct answer: steps toward representative government.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: One way in which the Declaration of Independence (1776) and the original United States Constitution (1789) are similar is that both documents

choices.text: guarantee voting rights to all persons
list grievances against the British monarchy
include a specific bill of rights
state that government receives its power from the people

questions.explanation: Correct answer: state that government receives its power from the people.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The presidential elections of 1876 and 2000 were controversial because the winner in both elections

choices.text: was a third-party candidate
had fewer popular votes than his opponent
carried fewer states than his opponent
had fewer electoral votes than his opponent

questions.explanation: Correct answer: had fewer popular votes than his opponent.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The decision in Marbury v. Madison (1803) was significant because it established that the Supreme Court

choices.text: had limited powers over state courts
could impeach the president and other government officials
had the power to choose its own members
could declare a federal law unconstitutional

questions.explanation: Correct answer: could declare a federal law unconstitutional.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Judicial review allows the Supreme Court to

choices.text: approve nominations to the president's cabinet
remove elected officials from office
determine the constitutionality of federal laws
oversee the financing of the lower federal courts

questions.explanation: Correct answer: determine the constitutionality of federal laws.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Which power regarding the federal judiciary was established in Marbury v. Madison?

choices.text: The president appoints all federal judges.
The Congress creates lower federal courts.
Federal laws may be declared unconstitutional.
Members of the federal courts serve life terms.

questions.explanation: Correct answer: Federal laws may be declared unconstitutional..

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The case of Marbury v. Madison (1803) established the principle that

choices.text: the states have power over the federal government
the president nominates federal judges
Congress can override presidential vetoes
the Supreme Court can declare federal laws unconstitutional

questions.explanation: Correct answer: the Supreme Court can declare federal laws unconstitutional.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What was an important impact of the McCulloch v. Maryland (1819) decision?

choices.text: It stated that the bicameral legislature established by the Constitution was illegal.
It determined that the creation of the National Bank was constitutional.
It established the idea that states should have the power to overturn federal laws.
It limited the power of Congress to add new states to the Union.

questions.explanation: Correct answer: It determined that the creation of the National Bank was constitutional..

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: One way in which the Declaration of Independence (1776) and the original United States Constitution (1789) are similar is that both documents

choices.text: state that government receives its power from the people
list grievances against the British monarchy
include a specific bill of rights
guarantee voting rights to all persons

questions.explanation: Correct answer: state that government receives its power from the people.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: One way in which the Square Deal, the New Frontier, and the Great Society are similar is that each was a

choices.text: campaign to extend woman's suffrage
plan to promote big business
presidential program of reform 49
book written by a muckraking journalist

questions.explanation: Correct answer: presidential program of reform 49.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What was a common goal of the Proclamation of Neutrality (1793), the Embargo Act (1807), and the Monroe Doctrine (1823)?

choices.text: encouraging independence movements in Latin America - 10th amendment, United States Constitution
avoiding conflicts with European nations
forcing Great Britain to grant independence to Canada
providing wartime aid to European nations

questions.explanation: Correct answer: avoiding conflicts with European nations.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: One goal of the Marshall Plan (1947) was to

choices.text: provide defensive weapons to Great Britain
rebuild the economy of Western Europe
bring Nazi war criminals to trial in Nuremberg
fund the construction of the iron curtain

questions.explanation: Correct answer: rebuild the economy of Western Europe.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The Roosevelt Corollary (1904) to the Monroe Doctrine proclaimed the right of the United States to

choices.text: determine the border between Alaska and Canada
intervene in the internal affairs of Latin American nations
impose trade restrictions on European imports
promote democracy in Africa

questions.explanation: Correct answer: intervene in the internal affairs of Latin American nations.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What was a primary purpose of the Marshall Plan?

choices.text: to support revolutions in the Middle East
to develop better relations with the Soviet Union
to contain the spread of communism
to expand United States territory

questions.explanation: Correct answer: to contain the spread of communism.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Congress enacted the Sherman Antitrust Act so that

choices.text: trade with foreign nations would be restricted
labor unions could bargain collectively
business practices that attempted to eliminate competition could be regulated
large businesses that hurt consumers could be nationalized by the government

questions.explanation: Correct answer: business practices that attempted to eliminate competition could be regulated.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The greatest weakness of the Sherman Antitrust Act was that it

choices.text: exempted companies that lobbied members of Congress
established fines that were considered unreasonable
denied individuals their right to trial if accused of violating the act
contained vague wording and could not be easily enforced

questions.explanation: Correct answer: contained vague wording and could not be easily enforced.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What is one impact of the Patriot Act on the United States?

choices.text: it increased international travel
it threatened civil liberties
it decreased militarization
it expanded immigration

questions.explanation: Correct answer: it threatened civil liberties.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: One way in which the Chinese Exclusion Act (1882) and the Gentlemen's Agreement (1907) are similar is that they

choices.text: led to an increase in Asian immigration
reflected nativist attitudes in the United States
eased requirements for citizenship
encouraged a policy of popular sovereignty

questions.explanation: Correct answer: reflected nativist attitudes in the United States.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Which point of view is being conveyed by the artist?

choices.text: opposition to the purchase of Alaska
praise for Manifest Destiny
encouragement of industrial development
opposition to the end of slavery

questions.explanation: Correct answer: praise for Manifest Destiny.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The policy described by Secretary Dulles was most evident in the later actions of the United States during the

choices.text: energy crisis of 1973
Camp David talks between Egypt and Israel $
Nuclear Test Ban Treaty
Cuban missile crisis

questions.explanation: Correct answer: Cuban missile crisis.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What was a major reason for President Harry Truman's June 27, 1950, statement?

choices.text: the United States commitment to the Cold War policy of containment
the continuing presence of Japanese forces in Korea after World War II
an attempt to gain the support of China to halt aggression against South Korea
the announcement of a plan to reunite North and South Korea

questions.explanation: Correct answer: the United States commitment to the Cold War policy of containment.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: In his 1902 message to Congress, President Theodore Roosevelt expressed support for the

choices.text: European colonization of Latin America
expansion of the United States as a world power
traditional United States foreign policy of isolationism
creation of a permanent United States military

questions.explanation: Correct answer: expansion of the United States as a world power.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What made the response to North Korea's invasion of South Korea different from past conflicts?

choices.text: Members of the United Nations supplied troops to defend South Korea.
The Soviet Union and the United States became allies.
The aggression of North Korea ended when the Soviet Union intervened.
Neighboring Southeast Asian nations were divided over which side to support.

questions.explanation: Correct answer: Members of the United Nations supplied troops to defend South Korea..

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What reason does Mercy Otis Warren give for the position she stated concerning the executive and legislative branches?

choices.text: Federal courts were a threat to individual liberty.
The thirteen states could never agree on important issues.
The duties of the president and of Congress were not clearly separated.
The United States Constitution would benefit only a wealthy few.

questions.explanation: Correct answer: The duties of the president and of Congress were not clearly separated..

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Anthony's statement is part of her campaign to gain

choices.text: woman's suffrage
a progressive income tax
consumer safety regulations
the abolition of slavery

questions.explanation: Correct answer: woman's suffrage.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What conclusion can be drawn from President Johnson's statement?

choices.text: President Johnson favored policies to deregulate industry.
American consumption of poultry was declining.
The federal government should take ownership of meatpacking plants.
President Johnson supported the expansion of consumer protection.

questions.explanation: Correct answer: President Johnson supported the expansion of consumer protection..

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: What was the primary reason President Andrew Jackson supported the removal of Native Americans from their tribal lands?

choices.text: it provided fertile farmland to Native Americans
it gave political support to Native Americans
it opened Native American land to white settlement
it granted citizenship to Native Americans

questions.explanation: Correct answer: it opened Native American land to white settlement.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Congress enacted the Sherman Antitrust Act so that

choices.text: trade with foreign nations would be restricted
business practices that attempted to eliminate competition could be regulated
large businesses that hurt consumers could be nationalized by the government
labor unions could bargain collectively

questions.explanation: Correct answer: business practices that attempted to eliminate competition could be regulated.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The decision of the Supreme Court in the case of United States v. Nixon (1974) stated that

choices.text: congressional approval of the War Powers Act of 1973 violated the separation of powers
executive branch officials could be fired by the president without the Senate’s approval
President Richard Nixon as commander in chief could extend the Vietnam War into Cambodia
executive privilege did not allow the president to withhold recordings that were related to the Watergate affair

questions.explanation: Correct answer: executive privilege did not allow the president to withhold recordings that were related to the Watergate affair.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: During the 1930s, poor land management and severe drought conditions across parts of the Midwest resulted in the

choices.text: development of Dust Bowl conditions on the Great Plains
formation of the United States Department of Agriculture
creation of wheat surpluses
growth of the Granger movement

questions.explanation: Correct answer: development of Dust Bowl conditions on the Great Plains.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: A major difference between the philosophies of President Herbert Hoover and President Franklin D. Roosevelt in responding to the Great Depression is that Roosevelt

choices.text: stressed the need for individual self-reliance
supported direct relief to people out of work
thought the government should not be involved in economic reform
wanted to rely on private charities to provide assistance

questions.explanation: Correct answer: supported direct relief to people out of work.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Which New Deal agency had the creation of new jobs as its primary goal?

choices.text: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC)
Works Progress Administration (WPA)
Agricultural Adjustment Administration (AAA)
Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)

questions.explanation: Correct answer: Works Progress Administration (WPA).

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: President Theodore Roosevelt was called a trustbuster because he

choices.text: encouraged conservation of natural resources
resigned from the Republican Party
directed the building of the Panama Canal
supported legal action against business monopolies

questions.explanation: Correct answer: supported legal action against business monopolies.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: Which presidential action is an example of the use of the unwritten constitution?

choices.text: vetoing a bill passed by Congress
nominating an ambassador to France
submitting a treaty to the Senate for ratification
holding a cabinet meeting at the White House

questions.explanation: Correct answer: holding a cabinet meeting at the White House.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: During the 1890s, many American farmers tried to resolve their economic problems by

choices.text: supporting government aid to railroads
joining the Populist Party
asking Congress to end agricultural subsidies
staging violent protests against the government

questions.explanation: Correct answer: joining the Populist Party.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: One way in which the Chinese Exclusion Act (1882) and the Gentlemen’s Agreement (1907) are similar is that they

choices.text: led to an increase in Asian immigration
eased requirements for citizenship
reflected nativist attitudes in the United States
encouraged a policy of popular sovereignty

questions.explanation: Correct answer: reflected nativist attitudes in the United States.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The Senate’s opposition to United States membership in the League of Nations was based mainly on the

choices.text: fear of being drawn into future wars
failure to give the United States veto power
cost of membership dues
concern that United States businesses would be damaged

questions.explanation: Correct answer: fear of being drawn into future wars.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The Great Compromise enabled delegates at the Constitutional Convention (1787) to

choices.text: provide for the indirect election of the president through the electoral college
protect the interests of states with small populations and states with large populations
establish the principle of popular sovereignty in the territories
give Congress the exclusive right to declare war if the nation is attacked

questions.explanation: Correct answer: protect the interests of states with small populations and states with large populations.

questions.subject: U.S. History
questions.stem: The decision in Marbury v. Madison (1803) was significant because it established that the Supreme Court

choices.text: had limited powers over state courts
could impeach the president and other government officials
had the power to choose its own members
could declare a federal law unconstitutional

questions.explanation: Correct answer: could declare a federal law unconstitutional.

questions.subject: Global History
questions.stem: Which major political event was partially caused by the Soviet war in Afghanistan?

choices.label: A
choices.text: building of the Berlin Wall

choices.label: B
choices.text: collapse of the Soviet Union

choices.label: C
choices.text: placement of nuclear missiles in Cuba

choices.label: D
choices.text: Soviet occupation of Hungary

questions.explanation: Correct answer: collapse of the Soviet Union.

questions.subject: Global History
questions.stem: What impact did Speakers C and D have on world history?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Their arguments led to the creation of labor unions and changes in working conditions.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Their arguments caused an immediate shift from absolutist rule to democratic rule.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Their ideas helped establish the foundation of the suffrage and anti-slavery movements.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Their ideas eased transitions from mercan- tilism to capitalism.

questions.explanation: Correct answer: Their ideas helped establish the foundation of the suffrage and anti-slavery movements..

questions.subject: Global History
questions.stem: Simón Bolívar’s actions were most likely in■ uenced by the ideas of

choices.label: A
choices.text: church of■ cials

choices.label: B
choices.text: Marxist followers

choices.label: C
choices.text: Enlightenment thinkers

choices.label: D
choices.text: laissez-faire economists

questions.explanation: Correct answer: Enlightenment thinkers.

questions.subject: Global History
questions.stem: Which issue are the Mothers of the Plaza de Mayo addressing?

choices.label: A
choices.text: the need for education reform

choices.label: B
choices.text: oppressive rule by a military dictatorship

choices.label: C
choices.text: a policy of ethnic cleansing

choices.label: D
choices.text: lack of job opportunities

questions.explanation: Correct answer: oppressive rule by a military dictatorship.

questions.subject: Global History
questions.stem: Marx and Engels are reacting to changes resulting from

choices.label: A
choices.text: industrialization

choices.label: B
choices.text: democratization

choices.label: C
choices.text: nationalism

choices.label: D
choices.text: imperialism

questions.explanation: Correct answer: industrialization.

questions.subject: Global History
questions.stem: Which historical development led Nehru to promote the policy of unaligned areas?

choices.label: A
choices.text: partitioning of India and Pakistan at independence

choices.label: B
choices.text: internal friction between various Indian ethnic groups

choices.label: C
choices.text: political pressure from his Parliament to pursue isolationism

choices.label: D
choices.text: expansion of Cold War blocs

questions.explanation: Correct answer: expansion of Cold War blocs.

sets: Grade 10 Global History II :: Conflicts + Human Rights Violations
Grade 10 Global History II :: Economic + Political Systems
Grade 10 Global History II :: Geography + Historical Turning Points
Grade 11 U.S. History :: Amendments + Documents + Laws
Grade 11 U.S. History :: Colonial + Constitution + Supreme Court
Grade 11 U.S. History :: Foreign Policy + Wars
Grade 11 U.S. History :: Modern Issues + Miscellaneous
Grade 11 U.S. History :: Presidents + Reformers + Movements
